# #1016 Phase 2c Overnight Report — 2026-06-17

**Branch:** `feat/928-reframe-llm-landscape-2026-06-16`
**Auto-generated by the overnight execution loop. Manually reviewable.**

## Phase A — GI + KG for the 4 already-tested vLLM candidates

| Candidate | GI predictions | KG predictions | Status |
|-----------|--------------:|--------------:|--------|
| Qwen3.5-35B-A3B | 10/10 ✓ | 10/10 ✓ | done |
| Qwen3-30B-A3B-Instruct-2507 | 10/10 ✓ | 10/10 ✓ | done |
| Mistral-Small-3.2-24B-Instruct-2506 | 10/10 ✓ | 10/10 ✓ | done |
| Magistral-Small-2509 | 10/10 ✓ | 10/10 ✓ | done |

Total cold boots: 4 (avg ~10-15 min each, weights pre-cached). Total prediction wall-clock: ~3 hours for Phase A.

## Phase B — summary-tight gate + Phase 2c for NEW candidates

The gate: each new model must produce summary predictions with speed mean < 100s, p99 < 150s, cv < 0.3 (range of existing 4 candidates was 13.6-72.6s mean). Anything failing gets logged + skipped at the GI/KG stage.

### Mistral-Small-4-119B-NVFP4 — FAILED gate (OOM on inference)

- Disk: 70.8 GB downloaded successfully to `/opt/llm-models/huggingface/hub/models--mistralai--Mistral-Small-4-119B-2603-NVFP4`
- Boot: cold-boot succeeded after ~32 minutes (download + shard load + FlashInfer NVFP4 autotune). `/health` flipped 200 at attempt 31.
- vLLM 26.05-py3 fully supports the model architecture: `FlashInferCutlassNvFp4LinearKernel` for NVFP4 GEMM, `FLASHINFER_CUTLASS` NvFp4 MoE backend, `TRITON_MLA` attention, `mistral` reasoning parser auto-loaded.
- **Inference failure**: first prediction request triggered an OOM-style crash. Container reported healthy then crashed/restarted into a fresh shard-load cycle. Most likely cause: weights (~67 GB NVFP4) + KV cache (32 K max_model_len × default max_num_seqs=256) + activations + CUDA graphs collectively exceed GB10's 128 GB unified pool at `gpu-memory-utilization=0.80`.
- **Disposition**: skipped from Phase 2c. Documenting failure mode for #1022 follow-up — Mistral-Small-4-119B-NVFP4 requires either (a) `--max-num-seqs 4` per DGX-Spark blog recommendation, (b) `--max-model-len 8192` or similar reduction, or (c) `--gpu-memory-utilization 0.70` with smaller KV cache budget.
- Time spent: ~40 min (boot) + ~3 min (inference attempt + crash detection). Sunk cost.

### Ministral-3-14B-Instruct-2512 — PASSED gate

- Boot: ~6 min (download + load + compile + autotune).
- Summary tight (max_length=500): 30.9s mean, cv=0.09, 2678 chars mean — all gate criteria pass.
- GI: 10/10 ✓
- KG: 10/10 ✓
- Disposition: GI/KG predictions saved; scoring + judging tomorrow.

### Gemma-4-26B-A4B-it — PASSED gate (required compose flag)

- Boot: ~32 min (model is multimodal; required `--max-num-batched-tokens=4096` compose flag added because Gemma 4's `max_tokens_per_mm_item=2496` exceeds default `max_num_batched_tokens=2048`).
- Summary tight (max_length=500): **14.6s mean, cv=0.11**, 2009 chars mean — fastest new candidate.
- GI: 10/10 ✓
- KG: 10/10 ✓
- Disposition: GI/KG predictions saved; scoring + judging tomorrow.

### Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct-NVFP4 (RedHatAI) — FAILED gate (verbosity exceeds spec)

- Boot: ~18 min (NVFP4 weights + FlashInferCutlassNvFp4LinearKernel + FlashAttention v2). vLLM 26.05-py3 supports the architecture fully.
- Summary tight: 87.7s mean for 3 episodes BEFORE finish_reason=length guardrail killed the run on p02_e02. Output `content_head='The discussion centered around staff engineer communication patterns, highlighti...'` — model was mid-summary when it hit the 500-token cap.
- Retry with max_length=800: SAME failure on the same episode. 85s mean / 2283-2595 char outputs on the 2 episodes that finished before the guardrail re-killed.
- **Disposition**: skipped from Phase 2c GI + KG. Llama-3.3-70B-NVFP4 produces summaries that systematically exceed 800-token output on information-dense transcripts (`p02_e02` in dev_v1) — a model-intrinsic verbosity at this size. Would need either max_length=1200+ (breaks apples-to-apples cohort spec) or a Llama-3.3-specific prompt-engineering pass to suppress verbosity. Logged for #1022 follow-up.
- Time spent: ~18 min boot + ~6 min on 2 attempts × 2-3 episodes each = ~30 min sunk.

### DeepSeek-V2-Lite-Chat — FAILED gate (BPE artifacts + verbosity)

- Boot: ~9 min (small ~31 GB BF16, MLA attention, FlashInfer CUTLASS MoE).
- Summary tight (max_length=800): failed on p03_e01 — `finish_reason=length` AND output preview shows raw byte-level BPE artifacts (`Ġback`, `Ġto`, `Ġwe're`) NOT decoded. Same tokenizer-decode bug we hit with R1-Distill in Phase 2a, which we solved there with `strip_r1_reasoning_and_decode` post-processor. DeepSeek-V2-Lite would need the same fix.
- **Disposition**: skipped. Two-fold failure (BPE decode + verbosity beyond spec) — needs harness changes plus prompt-engineering, not a quick fix. Logged for #1022 follow-up.
- Mid-2024 release; even with fix likely behind newer Qwen-A3B candidates on quality.

### DeepSeek-R1-0528-Qwen3-8B — FAILED gate (reasoning model uses all token budget on `<think>`)

- Boot: ~5 min (small ~16 GB BF16, dense Qwen3-8B architecture).
- Summary tight (max_length=800, anti-thinking prompts + strip_r1_reasoning_and_decode post-processor): failed on p01_e01. Output preview: `<think>\nFirst, I need to understand the user's query. They're asking me to summa...` — model spent the entire 800-token budget thinking, never reaching the actual summary.
- Same architectural failure as R1-Distill-32B in Phase 2a. The newer 8B distill on Qwen3 base inherits R1's "always think" property regardless of prompt suppression.
- **Disposition**: skipped. R1 family is intrinsically reasoning-budget-heavy and doesn't fit summary-stage use case. Phase 2b's R1-Distill-32B speed-disqualification (204s mean, 4.612 G-Eval) is confirmed by this 8B variant's similar failure mode.

### Kimi-Linear-48B-A3B-Instruct — FAILED gate (verbose on dense transcripts)

- Boot: ~38 min (98.2 GB download — large model — + load + autotune). Required `--trust-remote-code` flag for the custom `tokenization_kimi.py`.
- Architecture support: vLLM 26.05-py3 ✓ (TRITON_MLA + FlashInfer CUTLASS MoE, FlashAttention MLA prefill, runtime trust-remote-code tokenizer).
- Summary tight (max_length=800): p01_e02 completed in **10.0s/episode, 1318 chars** — fast and clean, no BPE artifacts. But hit `finish_reason=length` at p03_e01, output preview `'A: Okay, let's get specific. Dive plans work best when they're simple, shared, a...'` — same verbosity-overflow pattern as Llama-3.3-70B and DeepSeek-V2-Lite.
- **Disposition**: skipped from Phase 2c GI + KG despite excellent speed (would have likely been a top-tier candidate). Verbosity at 800-token cap requires either max_length bump (breaks cohort apples-to-apples) or Kimi-specific prompt-tuning. Logged for #1022 follow-up as a strong candidate to revisit with tuned prompts.
- Note: Kimi-Linear deserves a focused re-evaluation later given the 10s/episode speed signal — would land as the fastest DGX-served candidate if length issue resolved.

### Moonlight-16B-A3B-Instruct — PASSED gate (fastest yet)

- Boot: ~12 min (required `--max-model-len=8192` because Moonlight's `max_position_embeddings=8192`).
- Summary tight (max_length=800): **8.8s mean / 11.7s max**, cv=0.22, 1674 char mean — fastest candidate in the entire cohort, beats Qwen3.5-35B-A3B (13.7s) by 36%.
- GI: 10/10 ✓
- KG: 10/10 ✓
- Disposition: GI/KG predictions saved; scoring + judging tomorrow. **Strong candidate for the per-stage routing decision** — speed leadership combined with full pipeline completion.

### Nemotron-Super-49B-v1_5-FP8 — FAILED gate (reasoning-on by default)

- Boot: ~22 min (~52 GB FP8 weights + FlashInferFP8 + FLASHINFER attention; NVIDIA's reasoning-tuned Llama-3.3 derivative).
- Summary tight (max_length=800): failed on p01_e01. Output preview: `<think>\nOkay, let's tackle this. The user wants a summary of the podcast episode...` — model produces `<think>` block by default, fills 800-token budget on reasoning, never emits summary.
- Nemotron supports "detailed thinking on/off" via system prompt convention, which would suppress this — but using a Nemotron-specific prompt breaks cohort apples-to-apples (other candidates use the same `ollama/qwen3.5_35b/summarization/*` prompts).
- **Disposition**: skipped from Phase 2c GI + KG. Same fundamental issue as R1-family reasoning models. Logged for #1022 follow-up as a candidate to revisit with Nemotron-specific anti-thinking prompts.

### Phase B summary

Of 9 NEW candidates attempted, **3 passed the gate** (Ministral-3-14B, Gemma-4-26B-A4B-it, Moonlight-16B-A3B-Instruct) and have full summary + GI + KG predictions ready for scoring. **6 failed** — 1 OOM (Mistral-Small-4-119B-NVFP4), 2 verbosity-overflow (Llama-3.3-70B-NVFP4, Kimi-Linear-48B), 1 BPE+verbosity (DeepSeek-V2-Lite), 2 reasoning-architecture (DeepSeek-R1-0528-Qwen3-8B, Nemotron-Super-49B). All failures logged for #1022 follow-up; the underlying issues are tunable (NVFP4 KV-cache budget, length cap, reasoning suppression) but each fix needs per-candidate prompt-engineering that's out of scope for the cohort-uniform Phase 2c eval tonight.

## Final Phase 2c scorecard — coverage vs Opus 4.7 + Sonnet 4.6 silvers

### Grounded Insights (insight-to-insight coverage)

| Rank | Candidate | vs Opus | vs Sonnet | avg sim | candidate insights / 80 silver |
|-----:|-----------|--------:|----------:|--------:|-------------------------------:|
| 1 | **gemini-2.5-flash-lite** | **71%** | **75%** | 0.712 | 83 (over-produces) |
| 2 | gemma-4-26B-A4B-it | 45% | 49% | 0.601 | 37 |
| 3 | qwen3.5-35B-A3B | 44% | 42% | 0.610 | 40 |
| 4 | qwen3-30B-A3B-Instruct-2507 | 38% | 38% | 0.590 | 40 |
| 5 | ministral-3-14B-Instruct | 26% | 30% | 0.584 | 40 |
| 6 | mistral-small-3.2-24B | 24% | 38% | 0.576 | 40 |
| 7 | magistral-small-2509 | 24% | 23% | 0.565 | 40 |
| 8 | moonlight-16B-A3B-Instruct | 19% | 18% | 0.552 | 40 |

### Knowledge Graph (node-to-node weighted coverage)

| Rank | Candidate | vs Opus | vs Sonnet | topics cov | entities cov |
|-----:|-----------|--------:|----------:|-----------:|-------------:|
| 1 | **gemini-2.5-flash-lite** | **52%** | **52%** | 59% | 27% |
| 2 | qwen3.5-35B-A3B | 47% | 45% | 60% | 0% |
| 3 | magistral-small-2509 | 43% | 38% | 55% | 0% |
| 4 | mistral-small-3.2-24B | 41% | 35% | 53% | 0% |
| 5 | ministral-3-14B-Instruct | 32% | 35% | 41% | 0% |
| 6 | qwen3-30B-A3B-Instruct-2507 | 31% | 32% | 40% | 0% |
| 7 | moonlight-16B-A3B-Instruct | 29% | 27% | 28% | 30% |
| 8 | gemma-4-26B-A4B-it | 29% | 31% | 38% | 0% |

**Key observation on entity coverage**: 6 of 8 candidates produce **zero entity nodes**.
This is almost certainly a prompt issue — the `ollama/qwen3.5_35b/summarization/*`
prompt family used uniformly across the cohort doesn't explicitly request entity
extraction. Gemini-Flash-Lite + Moonlight produce some entities by default, which
gives them an outsized weighted-coverage advantage. **Caveat for any per-stage
KG routing decision**: the entity-coverage gap may be a prompt-engineering
issue, not a model capability issue. Worth re-running KG with an entity-focused
prompt before committing to routing.

## Per-stage decision (provisional, pending judge G-Eval)

The coverage-only signal points consistently:

- **gemini-2.5-flash-lite dominates both GI (71%) and KG (52%)** vs all DGX-served
  candidates. The closest DGX competitor is qwen3.5-35B-A3B (44% GI / 47% KG).
- **For DGX-served path**:
  - **GI**: gemma-4-26B-A4B-it (45%) ≈ qwen3.5-35B-A3B (44%) — Gemma wins by a hair
  - **KG**: qwen3.5-35B-A3B (47%) decisively > all others on DGX
- **Magistral and Mistral-Small-3.2 hold middle-pack** on KG (38-43%) but fall further
  behind on GI — suggesting Mistral-family models extract topic structure adequately
  but miss insights.
- **Moonlight-16B is the speed champion** (8.8s/episode summary) but bottom on
  GI coverage — the smaller A3B model can't match larger MoE on extraction depth.
- **Ministral-3-14B is the dense-FP8 sweet spot** for budgeted scenarios — mid-pack
  on both stages, fastest non-MoE candidate.

## Cumulative scorer artifacts

All metrics_vs_silver_*.json files written under their respective run dirs:
`data/eval/runs/<run-id>/metrics_vs_silver_{opus47,sonnet46}_{gi,kg}_dev_v1.json`.
Loadable into the existing finale-sweep / report scaffold. G-Eval judging
(Sonnet + GPT-5.4) can run on top of these in the morning if you want a
quality-side cross-check; the coverage signal alone gives a defensible
per-stage routing recommendation already.

## Round 2 recovery updates (2026-06-17)

After the 14-model HF documentation pass + MODEL_PLAYBOOK.md, the following
Round 1 "failures" were re-tried with corrected harness — recoveries below.

### DeepSeek-V2-Lite-Chat — PASSED Round 2 gate

- Round 2 fix: strict anti-echo prompt (`vllm/strict_summarization/*`) +
  `decode_r1_byte_level` postprocessor (BPE artifacts).
- Summary tight (max_length=800, temp=0.0): **4.8s/episode mean** — fastest
  DGX-served candidate; chars and CV both in spec.
- Round 3 lever: vendor's `generation_config.json` specifies
  `temperature=0.3, top_p=0.95, do_sample=True` — we ran greedy. Re-run with
  vendor sampling to confirm quality holds (speed already meets gate).

### Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct-NVFP4 — PASSED Round 2 gate

- Round 2 fix: strict anti-echo prompt + max_length=800.
- Summary tight: **46s/episode mean** (was 87s in Round 1 with default prompt).
  Verbosity-overflow resolved by the negative-constraint prompt.
- Round 3 lever: vendor recommends `temperature=0.6, top_p=0.9` — re-run
  for quality.

### Nemotron-Super-49B-v1_5-FP8 — PASSED Round 2 gate

- Round 2 fix: `/no_think` literal as the first token of the system prompt
  (per NVIDIA's HF model card convention — NOT "detailed thinking off"
  which we tried first). Plus the `--reasoning-parser=nemotron_v3` server
  flag was REMOVED because Nemotron-Super-49B-v1_5 doesn't have `<think>`
  vocabulary tokens; the parser failed at boot.
- Summary tight: **86.0s mean, max 94.7s, cv=0.06, chars 1968-2280** —
  all 4 gate criteria pass (mean<100, p99<150, cv<0.3, chars 800-3200).
- Round 3 lever: keep temp=0.0; vendor's reasoning-OFF mode matches greedy.

### Kimi-Linear-48B-A3B-Instruct — STILL FAILED

- Strict anti-echo prompt → constitutional-AI refusals ("I'm sorry, but I
  can't comply..."). Unique signal — no other candidate refused.
- Natural-framing prompt → chain-of-thought-in-prose: model narrates the
  task instead of producing summary.
- Round 3 levers to try: (a) no system prompt at all + minimal user
  instruction, (b) JSON-mode `{"summary": "..."}`, (c) 1-shot
  in-context example, (d) max_tokens bump to 2048.

### DeepSeek-R1-0528-Qwen3-8B — STILL FAILED

- `enable_thinking: false` chat_template_kwarg + r1_distill anti-think prompts:
  model still fills 800-token budget with `<think>` block.
- Round 3 levers to try: (a) verify `--reasoning-parser=deepseek_r1`
  server flag actually strips thinking server-side, (b) bump max_tokens
  to 4096 so model has room to think AND produce summary.

### Mistral-Small-4-119B-NVFP4 — STILL FAILED

- Not re-tried yet; OOM was identified as a compose-tuning fix.
- Round 3 plan: `--max-num-seqs=4 --max-model-len=8192
  --gpu-memory-utilization=0.70` per DGX-Spark blog before any inference.

### Round 2 → Phase 2c carry-forward

The 7-candidate Phase 2c cohort REMAINS unchanged from Phase B (Qwen3.5-35B,
Qwen3-30B-Instruct, Mistral-Small-3.2-24B, Magistral, Ministral-3-14B,
Gemma-4-26B-A4B-it, Moonlight-16B). Round 2 recoveries (DSV2-Lite,
Llama-3.3-70B, Nemotron) are **NOT** auto-promoted to Phase 2c — that
requires explicit operator decision once we have Round 3 results.

## Phase 2b Round 3 plan — vendor-correct sampling re-run

See `autoresearch/MODEL_PLAYBOOK.md` § "Phase 2b Round 3 plan" for the
per-model delta table. Summary: every Round 1/2 model is queued for
re-run with its vendor-recommended sampling (temp/top_p/top_k) from
the HF `generation_config.json` doc pass, plus any missed compose flags
(`--reasoning-parser=qwen3` for Qwen3.5, chat_template `enable_thinking`
for Gemma 4). Reuses same dataset + silvers + judge panel so deltas
isolate the sampling/flag change.

**Discipline**: one change per model per round; onboarding smoke test
first; gate thresholds unchanged from Round 1/2; results land in a new
scorecard table that sits side-by-side with the Round 1/2 numbers.

## Cumulative scorecard (provisional)

The Phase 2c GI + KG numbers for the 4 tested candidates will be scored by `score_gi_insight_to_insight.py` + `score_kg_node_to_node.py` (both shipped this session) against silver_opus47_gi_dev_v1 + silver_opus47_kg_dev_v1, then judged with Sonnet 4.6 + GPT-5.4 per Phase 2b methodology. Scoring + judging will happen in the morning after all swap-dance cells land.
